top_categories.workout_context: Dekom System Sudety Tour's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 93 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Dekom System Sudety Tour's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
88ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Dekom System Sudety Tour, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
8,200ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 93, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Dekom System Sudety Tour. Train it until it feels boring.
8,200ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Dekom System Sudety Tour, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 93 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Low-cadence force work for 8,200ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Dekom System Sudety Tour’s 93 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Dekom System Sudety Tour. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Dekom System Sudety Tour demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mountain passes at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 93 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 93 miles of mountain passes in Broumov, Czechia.
